Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The Collective Ambition Behind Odysseus, a Game-Changing Sci-Fi Larp 90 upvotes, 3 comments
  2. Z80 Sans – a disassembler in a font (2024) 19 upvotes, 0 comments
  3. LibreOffice: Request to the European Commission to adhere to its own guidances 218 upvotes, 34 comments
  4. FrameBook 237 upvotes, 43 comments
  5. Rijksmuseum researchers discover new painting by Rembrandt van Rijn 122 upvotes, 28 comments
  6. LibreOffice Writer now supports Markdown 135 upvotes, 33 comments
  7. What if the Apple ][ had run on Field-Sequential? 53 upvotes, 10 comments
  8. How to run Qwen 3.5 locally 406 upvotes, 135 comments
  9. I ported Linux to the PS5 and turned it into a Steam Machine 270 upvotes, 105 comments
  10. SWE-CI: Evaluating Agent Capabilities in Maintaining Codebases via CI 101 upvotes, 35 comments
  11. CLI RSS/Atom feed reader inspired by Taskwarrior, synced using Git 52 upvotes, 15 comments
  12. LLM Writing Tropes.md 260 upvotes, 123 comments
  13. Notes on writing Rust-based Wasm 162 upvotes, 76 comments
  14. Log messages are mostly for the people operating your software 55 upvotes, 23 comments
  15. I made a programming language with M&Ms 11 upvotes, 2 comments
  16. WSL Manager 13 upvotes, 3 comments
  17. Beagle, a source code management system that stores AST trees 61 upvotes, 32 comments
  18. Ask HN: Please restrict new accounts from posting 49 upvotes, 27 comments
  19. How important was the Battle of Hastings? 72 upvotes, 45 comments
  20. In vitro neurons learn and exhibit sentience when embodied in a game-world(2022) 10 upvotes, 3 comments
  21. Why can't you tune your guitar? (2019) 117 upvotes, 95 comments
  22. A basket of new fruit varieties is coming your way 18 upvotes, 10 comments
  23. The human.json Protocol 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  24. Pushing and Pulling: Three reactivity algorithms 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  25. Living human brain cells play DOOM on a CL1 [video] 73 upvotes, 68 comments
  26. Claude struggles to cope with ChatGPT exodus 88 upvotes, 85 comments
  27. My “grand vision” for Rust 59 upvotes, 54 comments
  28. Show HN: I built a real-time OSINT dashboard pulling 15 live global feeds 20 upvotes, 17 comments
  29. Ask HN: How to be alone? 68 upvotes, 81 comments
  30. Show HN: Skir – like Protocol Buffer but better 24 upvotes, 25 comments

Last generated 4:00am, 9 March 2026. Source here.
